title: The Man in the Desert

propositions.label: Others were involved in the chain of events leading to the man's death
The man's undressing was not forced by others
The matchstick was used as a tool to select a person among multiple people
Multiple people including the man were facing a survival crisis together
The man was in the air, not on the ground
The man's cause of death was not environmental (dehydration, heatstroke, or exhaustion)
The undressing was part of responding to a crisis that multiple people faced together
A match-drawing lottery was held among multiple people who were facing a survival crisis together

The man was riding in an airborne vehicle with several others when it began losing altitude; after everyone stripped and shed weight, a match-drawing lottery was held, and the man who was chosen jumped off and fell to his death
The man was with other people
There was a reason the man chose to undress
The matchstick was a tool used for a specific purpose between the man and others
The man and others were facing a shared difficulty
The man's voluntary undressing was related to the crisis they faced together
The selection of a person using matches was carried out as a response to the survival crisis
The man was traveling in some kind of vehicle
The man's death cannot be explained by simple environmental exposure alone
Weight reduction (including undressing) and a match-drawing lottery took place inside an airborne vehicle

The man was acting alone
The man's clothes were not taken from him by others
The man's clothes were taken from him by others

The man walked to the desert

The others were hostile toward the man

The matchstick was used as a signaling or communication tool

The man and others were engaged in routine activities

The undressing was for a personal reason unrelated to the crisis

The selection was part of a game or a bet

The man was traveling in a ground vehicle

The man was murdered

The vehicle crashed due to mechanical failure
The man chosen by the lottery died by a means other than being abandoned
The man chosen by the lottery was left to die of dehydration

statement: A naked man was found dead in the middle of the desert. He was clutching a broken matchstick in his hand.
truth: The man had been riding in a hot air balloon with several others when it began losing altitude. They threw out all their luggage and even stripped off their clothes to reduce weight, but it wasn't enough. They drew matches to decide who would jump off—the one who drew the short match. The man drew the short match, jumped, and fell to his death.
